commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `runCommandWithTimeout` to normalize timeout and no-output-timeout kills with missing or zero child exit codes to `124`, with Linux and Windows regression assertions updated to require that exact code.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests 0. Total 0 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can preserve `code: null` for timeout kills because only `0` is normalized to `124`, and the PR's focused tests model that exact killed-child path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the patch has no blocking findings, but a member-authored PR that changes plugin-facing process result semantics needs maintainer acceptance.

target.title: fix(process): return timeout code for killed commands
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `runCommandWithTimeout` could return `code: null` after killing a timed-out child process, even while preserving partial stdout.
- Why it matters: plugin callers that normalize a missing process code to `0` can accidentally treat timeout output as success and cache partial output.
- What changed: timeout and no-output-timeout terminations now normalize a missing or zero child code to `124`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not optimize `openclaw recipes list`, change launchd scheduling, or modify the external recipes/kitchen plugin flow.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #73743
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a timed-out `runtime.system.runCommandWithTimeout` child process now returns a concrete nonzero timeout code instead of `null`, so partial stdout cannot look successful to callers that only inspect the numeric code.
- Real environment tested: Docker `node:22-bookworm`, Node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.2.2, patched OpenClaw checkout mounted at `/repo`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
docker run --rm \
  -v <patched-openclaw-checkout>:/repo \
  -v <timeout-proof-script>:/tmp/openclaw-timeout-proof.mts \
  -w /repo node:22-bookworm \
  sh -lc 'corepack enable && pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-timeout-proof.mts'
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "code": 124,
  "termination": "timeout",
  "noOutputTimedOut": false,
  "killed": true,
  "stdout": "ready",
  "signal": "SIGKILL"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the real child process printed partial stdout, was killed by the timeout, and the runtime result carried `termination: "timeout"` with `code: 124`.
- What was not tested: full macOS launchd recipes fan-out and ClawKitchen cache poisoning were not reproduced locally; this focused proof covers the shared process-runtime timeout result used by plugin runtime callers.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing tests only asserted timeout code was not zero; the implementation could still return `null` for a timeout kill when the child had no numeric exit code.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: timeout termination preserved `termination: "timeout"` but only converted `0` to `124`; a killed child with no numeric exit code stayed `null`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: timeout regression tests did not assert the exact numeric code returned to callers.
- Contributing context (if known): #73743 describes a plugin caller normalizing missing process status fields to success while caching partial subprocess output.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/process/exec.test.ts`, `src/process/exec.windows.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: overall timeout and no-output timeout return `code: 124` when the killed child does not provide a numeric exit code.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the shared process result normalization, before plugin-specific callers receive the result.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing timeout tests covered termination classification but not the exact timeout code.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Timeout and no-output-timeout command results now report `code: 124` when the killed child has no numeric exit code. Non-timeout signal exits still preserve `code: null`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[child times out] -> [SIGKILL] -> [code: null] -> [caller may coerce to success]

After:
[child times out] -> [SIGKILL] -> [code: 124] -> [caller sees nonzero timeout]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: command timeout result semantics are stricter only for timeout/no-output-timeout cases. The existing `termination` field remains unchanged, and non-timeout signal exits still preserve `code: null`.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container on Docker
- Runtime/container: `node:22-bookworm`, Node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.2.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Ran a real child process through `runCommandWithTimeout`.
2. The child wrote `ready` to stdout and then stayed alive.
3. `runCommandWithTimeout` killed it at the configured timeout.

### Expected

- The result reports timeout termination with a nonzero numeric timeout code.

### Actual

- `code: 124`
- `termination: "timeout"`
- `stdout: "ready"`
- `signal: "SIGKILL"`

### Verification commands

```sh
docker run --rm \
  -v <patched-openclaw-checkout>:/repo \
  -w /repo node:22-bookworm \
  sh -lc 'corepack enable && OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm test src/process/exec.test.ts src/process/exec.windows.test.ts'
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       29 passed (29)
```
